business.name: Northbrook Reproductive Health
business.stack_summary: Single-site fertility clinic. 6 providers. ~40 IVF cycles/month. Bedrock-only.

pantry.explanation: Clinical notes export to DOCX. PHI. The conversion hook turns each note into clean markdown, but ONLY in the PHI-scoped clinical/ folder. Cannot leak.

connection_methods.name: eCW REST API (or HL7 for older tenants)
connection_methods.when_to_use: Required, no CLI, no MCP for eCW.
cons: eCW API access requires vendor approval
HL7 is verbose for older tenants
connection_methods.install: Vendor-provided API key + endpoint. ECW_API_KEY in ~/.env. Token must stay in the Bedrock-tenant only.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Skill watches a vendor-provided drop folder; converts DOCX clinical notes to structured markdown under data/clinical/. Path-scoped rule prevents the agent from loading these in non-clinical contexts.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Eliminates the manual DOCX export every morning. Currently ~30 min.

pantry.explanation: Billing + scheduling. ERA reconciliation here. Marisol exports XLSX every Friday. Not PHI-tagged in the same way as clinical notes, billing context is allowed cross-domain only via the Billing-Appeals Specialist's controlled bridge.

connection_methods.name: Centricity REST API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Pulls billing entries + ERA files programmatically.
cons: Centricity API is dated; auth is per-tenant

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Pulls weekly ERA + billing entries. Marisol stops exporting manually.

pantry.explanation: Tablet intake at the front desk. Scanned to PDF, lives in SharePoint. Becca pulls each morning and walks them down the hall. Goes into data/raw_dropzone/ for the conversion hook to handle.

connection_methods.name: Local file + conversion hook
connection_methods.when_to_use: Drop PDFs into data/raw_dropzone/ each morning (or schedule a SharePoint sync).
cons: Becca has to remember to drop the files (or build a sync)

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Reads converted intake markdown + cross-references with eCW for returning patients. Writes silver_platters/intake_pipeline_<week>.md.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Becca's 90-minute morning print-and-walk routine drops to 10 minutes of review

pantry.explanation: AMH, semen analyses, hormone panels. Lab summarizer pulls overnight, flags abnormals only.

connection_methods.name: Lab vendor's HL7 / FHIR feed
connection_methods.when_to_use: If your lab supports FHIR. Most modern labs do.
connection_methods.install: Vendor-provided FHIR endpoint. Token managed via the EHR integration layer.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Reads lab PDFs (or FHIR feed). Tags LOW / HIGH / NORMAL with provider review status. Only abnormals surface in morning briefs.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Providers stop clicking through 14 panels per patient. ~20 min/provider/morning.

pantry.explanation: Marisol's appeal templates. Today she writes each appeal from scratch (4-5 hours each). The drafter cuts it to days.

connection_methods.name: Local file + conversion hook
connection_methods.when_to_use: Templates live in SharePoint; drop into raw_dropzone/.
cons: No automation on the fax side, that's the human's job

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: (no extraction skill needed, drafted by the Billing-Appeals Specialist)
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Templates feed into the appeal drafter. The agent fills in patient case + denial reason + clinical justification + medical society citation.

pantry.explanation: Provider + tech schedule. Cross-referenced for morning briefs ('which provider is on which day?'). Not PHI-tagged.

connection_methods.name: Mark's gws (Google Workspace) CLI
connection_methods.when_to_use: Already wrapped at ~/.claude/skills/gws-cli/. Pulls Sheets directly.

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: Use gws directly to pull schedule into morning brief flow
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: No new skill needed. The morning brief reads the schedule via gws.

pantry.explanation: Read into rules/phi_scoping.md as the always-on PHI rule. Updated only when policy changes (rare).

connection_methods.name: Local file (read once into rule)
connection_methods.when_to_use: Read once into the rule body. Don't re-read every session.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: n/a, content lives in the path-scoped rule itself.

pantry.explanation: Dr. Mehra's BAA with Anthropic + Bedrock deployment is THE unlock. Without it, no PHI touches AI. With it, every skill / agent / hook runs PHI-safely.

connection_methods.name: AWS Bedrock invokeModel API
connection_methods.install: AWS account + Bedrock model access. AWS_PROFILE in ~/.env. Configure Claude Code backend.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Already configured for the clinic. Required precondition for everything below.

prep.name: intake_pipeline_<week>.md
prep.explanation: New patients this week + returning patients with status changes. Becca reads this once instead of printing each form.
prep.sample_content:
# Intake Pipeline, W44

## New patients (3)
| MRN | Initials | DOB | Reason | Provider | Day 1 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| NB-44218 | JC | 1989-04-12 | Secondary infertility, post-cycle 1 IUI failed | Dr. Mehra | 2025-11-03 |
| NB-44263 | MS | 1986-07-30 | Repeat SA after abnormal initial | Dr. Mehra | 2025-11-03 |

## Insurance / billing flags (clinical-impacting)
- NB-43009 (SS): IVF cycle denial CO-167 (experimental). Active appeal with Aetna. Cycle is mid-stim, do NOT pause based on billing.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt:
---
paths:
  - data/intake/**
  - data/clinical/**
  - data/labs/**
  - silver_platters/intake_*.md
  - outputs/morning_briefs/**
---

# PHI Scoping, THE HARD RULE

Patient health information stays in clinical, intake, labs paths. Billing / scheduling / admin contexts NEVER load PHI in the same session, except via the Billing-Appeals Specialist's controlled cross-domain pathway.
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Patient intake brief Claude writes, Mon 6am

prep.name: lab_review_pending.md
prep.explanation: Tagged LOW / HIGH / NORMAL with provider review status. Only abnormals surface in morning brief.
prep.sample_content:
# Lab Review Queue, 2025-11-03 (overnight)

## Abnormal, provider sign-off needed
- JC / NB-44218, AMH 0.84 ng/mL, LOW for age (DOR). Dr. Mehra to review.
- MS / NB-44263, SA repeat, persistent asthenospermia. Refer urology.

## Trending normal (no action)
4 routine in-range labs auto-confirmed.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt: (governed by phi_scoping.md, always-on for clinical paths)
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Lab results brief Claude writes, Daily 5:30am

prep.name: billing_reconciliation_<week>.md
prep.explanation: ERA mismatches flagged. Doesn't touch the books. Marisol reviews Friday afternoon.
prep.sample_content:
# Billing Reconciliation, W44

## ERA mismatches (3)
- CL-9822 (JC AMH), denied CO-50, allowed $75 not paid. APPEAL candidate.
- CL-9825 + CL-9826 (SS IVF cycle), denied CO-167 'experimental.' APPEAL urgent, pre-auth was approved before service.

## Patient-deductible holds
- CL-9824 (MS SA), PR-1, $142 to patient.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt:
---
paths:
  - data/billing/**
  - silver_platters/billing_*.md
---

# Billing Codes Rules
- CO-167 'experimental' is appeal-worthy if pre-auth was approved.
- Cross-domain: only Billing-Appeals Specialist may join billing + clinical.
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Billing brief Claude writes, Fri 5pm

prep.name: insurance_appeal_status.md
prep.explanation: Active appeals list. Controlled cross-domain (clinical + billing) for the appeal drafter only.
prep.sample_content:
# Insurance Appeal Status, W44

## Active appeals (3)
- JC / NB-44218 / Aetna / CL-9822 (CO-50 AMH denial), drafted, Marisol reviewing.
- SS / NB-43009 / Aetna / CL-9825+9826 (CO-167 IVF), URGENT. Pre-auth was approved. Drafted today.
- AB / NB-42855 / Cigna / CL-9788 (CO-97 bundling), pending Marisol's research.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt: (governed by billing_codes.md, controlled cross-domain via the Billing-Appeals Specialist only)
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Insurance appeals brief Claude writes, Weekly

plate.name: Morning brief per provider
plate.explanation: Today's patients, intake highlights, lab flags, action items. Initials + MRN, never full names. Replaces Becca's 90-minute print-and-walk.
plate.sample_output:
# Morning Brief, Dr. AK, 2025-11-03

Today's clinic: 9 patients (3 new, 6 returning). 2 retrievals.

## New patients
### JC / NB-44218 (8:00 AM)
- 36yo, secondary infertility eval, IUI failed.
- AMH back this weekend: 0.84 ng/mL, LOW (DOR).
- Action: discuss accelerated IVF; sign off AMH in EMR.

## Pending labs (3)
- JC AMH, LOW, your sign-off.
ideation_loop: Silver platters update: intake_pipeline_<week>.md and lab_review_pending.md.
Care Coordinator Orchestrator confirms PHI scope (clinical only). Routes to Intake-Prep Specialist.
Intake-Prep reads intake_pipeline + lab_review + (via gws) staff_schedule.
For each patient on the provider's day, assembles: initials/MRN, reason for visit, lab flags, action items.
Provider reads on iPad before rounds. Initials at top of file. [provider_action] line in audit_log.
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by the intake-prep specialist for Dr. Mehra, providers

plate.name: Insurance appeal letter draft
plate.approval_gate: Marisol reviews + Dr. Mehra co-signs before fax
plate.explanation: Drafts a 7-section appeal letter from billing denial + clinical justification. The ONLY agent allowed cross-domain access, and only for this purpose.
plate.sample_output:
# Appeal, JC / NB-44218, Aetna, CL-9822 (CO-50)

## 1. Patient identifier
JC / NB-44218 / Aetna POS / Group 884442

## 2. Date of service
2025-10-29 / CPT 82397 (AMH)

## 3. Denial reason
CO-50, Medical necessity not met

## 4. Clinical justification
36yo G1P1 with secondary infertility, TTC 14 months. AMH testing is standard-of-care in evaluation of diminished ovarian reserve per ASRM Practice Committee guidelines.

## 5. Medical society citation
ASRM Practice Committee, 'Testing and interpreting measures of ovarian reserve' (Fertil Steril 2020;114:1151).
ideation_loop: Friday 5pm: Centricity ERA file produces a denial. ingest_centricity_billing flags CL-9822 in billing_reconciliation_<week>.md.
Care Coordinator Orchestrator routes to Billing-Appeals Specialist (the ONLY agent cleared for cross-domain).
Billing-Appeals reads the denial in billing_reconciliation. Identifies appeal grounds.
Billing-Appeals reads relevant clinical context (encounter notes for the service date), strictly the minimum needed.
Drafts the 7-section appeal letter per the firm template. Saves to outputs/insurance_appeals_drafts/<initials>_<carrier>_<date>.md.
Marisol reviews. Dr. Mehra co-signs (clinical justification accuracy).
Marisol faxes (the human's job). Audit log captures draft + both approvals.
plate.approval_friendly: Marisol reviews + Dr. Mehra co-signs before sending
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by the billing-appeals specialist for Marisol, Dr. Mehra

recipes.name: Morning brief per provider (5:30am)
recipes.goal: Each provider opens their iPad before rounds and sees today's patients, lab flags, and action items, initials + MRN only. No more Becca printing forms and walking them down the hall.

walkthrough.action: Triggers ingest_intake_pdf + ingest_ecw_notes + lab_result_summarizer.
Update intake_pipeline_<week>.md and lab_review_pending.md silver platters.
Triggers Care Coordinator Orchestrator.
Confirms domain = clinical. PHI-scoping rule loads. Routes to Intake-Prep Specialist.
Reads silver platters + (via gws skill) the day's staff schedule.
For each patient on each provider's day, assembles: initials + MRN (never full name in headers), reason for visit, lab flags, action items, insurance flags affecting today's care.
Drafts outputs/morning_briefs/<date>_<provider>.md per provider.
Logs every read to outputs/audit_log.md (the audit log is itself PHI; stays in Bedrock tenant).
Opens brief on iPad. Initials at top. [provider_action] line in audit_log.

recipes.before_claude_code: Becca prints intake forms each morning, hand-summarizes for providers, walks them down the hall. 90 minutes daily. Providers click through 14 lab panels per cycle patient.
recipes.after_claude_code: Brief on iPad at 6am. 90 minutes back to clinical work. Providers see only abnormals.
recipes.headline: Morning brief per provider (5:30am)

recipes.name: Lab review queue (overnight)
recipes.goal: Abnormal labs get flagged for provider sign-off before they walk in. Routine in-range labs auto-confirm. Providers stop clicking through 14 panels per patient.

walkthrough.action: Drops result PDFs (or FHIR feed) into data/raw_dropzone/.
Converts each lab PDF into structured markdown in data/labs/.
Reads each lab. Compares to reference range for patient age + sex.
Tags each panel: LOW / HIGH / NORMAL. Cross-references with patient's prior labs to spot trends.
Writes silver_platters/lab_review_pending.md with abnormals at top, normals collapsed.
Sees abnormals in morning brief. Reviews + signs off in EMR. Routine labs auto-confirm in audit log.

recipes.before_claude_code: Providers click through 14 panels per cycle patient before AM rounds. ~20 min/provider/morning.
recipes.after_claude_code: Only abnormals surface. Provider review takes 5 min.
recipes.headline: Lab review queue (overnight)

recipes.name: Insurance appeal draft (when denial lands)
recipes.goal: Marisol stops writing appeals from scratch. Each denial triggers a drafted letter with the 7 required sections within hours. Marisol reviews, Dr. Mehra co-signs, fax.

walkthrough.actor: Friday 5pm, Centricity ERA file produces a denial
walkthrough.action: ingest_centricity_billing flags the denial in billing_reconciliation_<week>.md.
Triggers Care Coordinator Orchestrator.
Detects this is the controlled cross-domain pathway (appeal). Routes to Billing-Appeals Specialist.
Reads the denial entry in billing_reconciliation. Identifies appeal grounds (CO-50, CO-167, etc.).
Reads relevant clinical context, minimum-necessary: encounter notes for the service date, lab results that document medical necessity. PHI scope active throughout.
Drafts the 7-section appeal letter per the firm template: patient ID + DOS + denial reason + clinical justification + medical society citation + pre-auth reference + escalation request.
Saves to outputs/insurance_appeals_drafts/<initials>_<carrier>_<date>.md. Adds [pending_marisol_review] line to audit_log.
Reviews the draft. Edits as needed.
Co-signs (clinical justification accuracy). Adds [provider_action] line to audit_log.
Faxes (the human's job). Tracks confirmation.

recipes.before_claude_code: Marisol writes each appeal from scratch. 4-5 hours per appeal. 6-8 weeks to resolution. Many denials never get appealed because she runs out of time.
recipes.after_claude_code: Draft ready within hours of denial landing. Marisol's review = 30 min. More denials get appealed. More money recovered.
recipes.headline: Insurance appeal draft (when denial lands)

recipes.name: Weekly clinic P&L (Friday close)
recipes.goal: Dr. Mehra opens her phone Friday afternoon and sees clinic P&L for the week, collections, denials, outstanding A/R, payroll burden. Today she has no single weekly view.

walkthrough.action: Triggers ingest_centricity_billing for the week's collections + denials.
Triggers Care Coordinator Orchestrator.
Routes to a weekly_pnl_assembler skill (lightweight, no specialist needed).
Reads billing_reconciliation + staff schedule. Computes: gross collections, denials outstanding, A/R aging, payroll burden estimate.
Drafts outputs/clinic_weekly_pnl_<week>.md. NOT auto-distributed. Read-only.
Opens on phone. Strategic visibility.

recipes.before_claude_code: Dr. Mehra has no single weekly view of clinic financial performance. Reads end-of-month from Marisol.
recipes.after_claude_code: Weekly P&L on Friday. 5-min read. Strategic decisions land sooner.
recipes.headline: Weekly clinic P&L (Friday close)

setup_priority.title: Confirm Bedrock + BAA deployment is live
setup_priority.what_to_do: Verify the AWS Bedrock 3P Claude tenant is active, BAA with Anthropic is signed, and audit-log retention policy is in place.
setup_priority.why: PHI cannot touch Claude any other way. This is the IT precondition. Already done for Dr. Mehra, verify before building.
setup_priority.install:
# Verify configuration:
echo $ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL
echo $AWS_PROFILE
# Confirm BAA filed with Anthropic legal.
# Confirm audit log retention policy (typically 6 years for HIPAA).
setup_priority.before: PHI cannot touch AI. Tony pulls the plug on any pilot.
setup_priority.setup_time: 1-2 hours of verification (assuming BAA already signed)
setup_priority.title_friendly: Confirm Bedrock + BAA deployment is live

setup_priority.title: Lock the PHI-scoping rule (rules/phi_scoping.md)
setup_priority.what_to_do: Write rules/phi_scoping.md with paths: data/clinical/**, data/intake/**, data/labs/**, outputs/morning_briefs/**, outputs/insurance_appeals_drafts/**.
setup_priority.why: Clinical and billing data physically cannot mix in the same session, except via the Billing-Appeals Specialist's controlled bridge. File system enforces what hope used to.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/rules/phi_scoping.md
# YAML frontmatter:
# ---
# paths:
#   - data/clinical/**
#   - data/intake/**
#   - data/labs/**
#   - outputs/morning_briefs/**
# ---
# Body: PHI scoping rules + minimum-necessary principle (template in demos/04_healthcare_clinic/after/.claude/rules/)
setup_priority.before: PHI scoping enforced by 'be careful' instinct. HIPAA risk.
setup_priority.after: Agent reading data/billing/ literally cannot load data/clinical/ in the same session. Walling enforced by file system.

setup_priority.title: Wire the conversion hook + ingest_intake_pdf + lab_result_summarizer
setup_priority.what_to_do: SessionStart hook for raw_dropzone. Then write ingest_intake_pdf and lab_result_summarizer skills.
setup_priority.why: Intake PDFs and lab PDFs are the highest-volume PHI inputs. Without ingestion, the morning brief has nothing to read.
setup_priority.install:
# 1. Hook setup
brew install pandoc poppler && pip install xlsx2csv
# 2. Skills
# write under .claude/skills/ingest_intake_pdf/ and .claude/skills/lab_result_summarizer/
# Both write to PHI-scoped folders only.
setup_priority.before: Becca prints + walks intake forms. Providers click through 14 lab panels per patient.
setup_priority.after: Daily 5:30am, intake_pipeline + lab_review silver platters refresh. Brief ready 6am.
setup_priority.title_friendly: Wire the conversion hook + ingest_intake_pdf + lab_result_summarizer

setup_priority.title: Care Coordinator Orchestrator + Intake-Prep + Lab-Review + Billing-Appeals specialists
setup_priority.what_to_do: Define four subagents under .claude/agents/. Care Coordinator is the chief-of-staff. Three specialists, each scoped to its domain. Billing-Appeals has the controlled cross-domain pathway.
setup_priority.why: Domain scoping enforces minimum-necessary. Becca and Dr. Mehra talk to the orchestrator; specialists report up.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/agents/:
#   care-coordinator-orchestrator.md (model: opus, tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Task])
#   intake-prep-specialist.md (model: sonnet, scoped to clinical+intake)
#   lab-review-specialist.md (model: sonnet, scoped to labs+clinical)
#   billing-appeals-specialist.md (model: opus, controlled cross-domain, only allowed for appeals)
# Hand to @claude-code-guide for templates.
setup_priority.before: Becca asks a question, has to pre-frame which folder Claude should look at.
setup_priority.after: Becca runs /morning_brief or /draft_appeal. Orchestrator routes per minimum-necessary scope.
setup_priority.title_friendly: Care Coordinator Orchestrator + Intake-Prep + Lab-Review + Billing-Appeals specialists

setup_priority.title: Audit log + retention + break-glass procedure
setup_priority.what_to_do: PostToolUse hook captures every action. Stop hook warns on unsigned drafts. Define break-glass procedure for emergency cross-domain queries (medical-director-only override).
setup_priority.why: HIPAA audit-log retention is non-negotiable. Stop hook is the human-gate. Break-glass is for clinical emergencies that justify cross-domain access.
setup_priority.install:
# hooks per business_os_hooks.md (already written)
# define break-glass: a slash command /break_glass that requires Dr. Mehra's explicit invocation + writes a special audit_log entry
setup_priority.before: AI did what? Nobody knows. Did Dr. Mehra approve this morning's brief? No record.
setup_priority.after: Every action recorded. Stop hook nudges if a draft is unsigned. Break-glass is auditable.

setup_total_time: Bedrock + BAA is the precondition (already done). Beyond that, ~3-4 days of focused work, mostly the skill writing.

opportunities.title: Bedrock + BAA setup walkthrough for clinics
opportunities.explanation: Clinic-specific Bedrock setup, BAA template, audit-log review cadence, break-glass procedure live in the Early AI Dopters community as a deeper dive.

opportunities.title: Lock the PHI scoping rule
opportunities.explanation: The rule is the file-system enforcement of HIPAA minimum-necessary. Without it, every other step is theoretical.

opportunities.title: Write an EHR-export-to-markdown skill
opportunities.explanation: eClinicalWorks doesn't have a clean export. The skill watches a vendor-provided drop folder and converts DOCX clinical notes to structured markdown.
